author: lachiejames
text:
> Canonical public roadmap for v1.0.0 of SlopWeaver. Pinned to the repo so contributors and curious onlookers can see what's being built. Each checkbox links to its own sub-issue (or PR) as work progresses.

**Status**: pre-alpha. v1.0.0 ships when the items below are all done. No fixed date — ships when ready.

**v1.0.0 hook**: _Help Claude Code answer "what should I work on next?" by searching across your work tools._ See [README](../blob/main/README.md) for the demo scenario.

> **Auth model**: stdio MCP transport only; no MCP-layer auth. See [resolution comment on #11](https://github.com/slopweaver/slopweaver/issues/11) and the amendments comment below for the full reasoning. The original lines under each section have been updated to reflect the post-#11 reality.

---

## CLI commands

- [ ] `npx -y slopweaver@latest init` — first-run interactive setup (#39)
- [ ] `slopweaver doctor` — diagnostic checks wired into the binary (`pnpm cli doctor` already exists for maintainers)
- [x] `slopweaver connect github` — GitHub fine-grained PAT (#34)
- [x] `slopweaver connect slack` — Slack bot token paste (#34)
- [x] `slopweaver` (default command) — runs the local server with **stdio MCP transport**; web UI on `localhost:60701` is a separate concern (#28)
- [ ] `slopweaver status` — service health + per-integration freshness

## MCP tools (5 core + 2 diagnostic)

- [x] `start_session()` — **THE FLAGSHIP**. Searches across all integrations, returns prioritized list with reasoning (#35)
- [ ] `get_context_on(reference)` — generic "what is this?" investigation across integrations
- [ ] `get_pr_context(pr_url)` — specific case of `get_context_on` for PRs
- [ ] `catch_me_up(since)` — what changed while offline (#38)
- [ ] `search_work_context(query, filters?)` — full-text search over local evidence log (#38)
- [ ] `get_freshness()` — per-integration last-poll + stale flag (#38)
- [x] `ping()` — connection verification (#27)

## Local web UI on `localhost:60701`

- [x] **Diagnostics** page — first-class onboarding UI; last poll times, integration health, MCP client connection status (#36)
- [ ] **Session view** — what `start_session()` returned most recently, browsable
- [ ] **Evidence Log Browser** — full-text search + filter + citation chips
- [ ] **Inbox** — unified deduplicated feed (minimal styling)
- [ ] **Settings** — integrations connect/disconnect, telemetry toggle

> Web UI is web-UI-only on `localhost:60701` — not an MCP transport. MCP clients use stdio (per #11).

## Packages (in roughly this order)

- [x] `packages/contracts/` — Zod tool schemas (#24)
- [x] `packages/env/` — Zod env-var validation (#30)
- [x] `packages/db/` — Drizzle schemas (`evidence_log`, `identity_graph`, `integration_state`, `workspaces`, `integration_tokens`) + SQLite migrations (#25)
- [x] `packages/mcp-server/` — framework-agnostic MCP server (stdio), tool registry (#27)
- [x] `packages/integrations/github/` — GitHub polling for PRs/issues/mentions (#33)
- [x] `packages/integrations/slack/` — Slack polling for mentions/DMs (#31)
- [x] `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/composite/start-session.ts` — flagship composite tool (#35)
- [x] `packages/ui/` — React UI for the local web UI (#36)
- [x] `apps/mcp-local/` — CLI binary wiring everything (#28)
- [ ] **Decision-record open**: `integration_tokens` encryption at rest (#37) — keychain vs AES-on-disk vs env

## Security

- [x] gitleaks pre-commit + CI gate (#32)
- [ ] `integration_tokens` encryption at rest (#37) — current state: plaintext, deferred from #34. Blocks public launch.

## OSS infrastructure

- [x] `CONTRIBUTING.md` — short stub (#4)
- [x] `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` — Contributor Covenant 2.1 (#4)
- [x] `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{task,decision_record,bug_report,feature_request,integration_request}.yml` (#4, #9)
- [x] `.github/pull_request_template.md` (#4)
- [x] GitHub Actions: `ci.yml` for format + lint + knip + compile + test on every PR (#6, #17)
- [x] GitHub Actions: `release.yml` for npm publish on GitHub Release (#14)

## Pre-launch (founder action items)

- [x] Reserve `slopweaver` npm name (v0.0.0 stub published)
- [x] New public repo created
- [ ] GitHub OAuth App for SlopWeaver Local (only if/when OAuth replaces PAT for GitHub user-auth — currently PAT-only is fine)
- [ ] Slack App for SlopWeaver Local (bot token scopes)
- [ ] GitHub Sponsors application

## Launch day

- [ ] 60–90 second launch video (terminal + Claude Code + result)
- [ ] `server.json` + Submit to Official MCP Registry
- [ ] Submit to PulseMCP
- [ ] Submit to ≥2 awesome-mcp lists
- [ ] Publish Claude Code plugin marketplace.json (`/plugin marketplace add slopweaver/slopweaver`)
- [ ] Show HN (Tuesday/Wednesday US Eastern morning)
- [ ] X / Bluesky thread (founder voice — pivot lessons + demo)
- [ ] Discord cross-posts (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, MCP communities)

---

## Success metric

**100 users who successfully call `start_session` from an MCP client within 30 days of launch.** Stars are top of funnel; user-completing-the-flow is the real bottom-of-funnel metric.

---

## Out of scope for v1.0.0

- ❌ HTTP MCP transport (year 2 cloud tier — Streamable HTTP + OAuth 2.1+PKCE)
- ❌ MCP-layer auth (`slopweaver token create / list / revoke`, `packages/auth/`, MCP bearer tokens) — dropped per #11
- ❌ Named agents (no agent runtime in v1; Claude Code IS the agent)
- ❌ Mutations (read-only MCP tools only)
- ❌ Mobile / push notifications (year 2 cloud-tier)
- ❌ Cross-device sync (year 2)
- ❌ Webhook ingestion (year 2 — v1 polls)
- ❌ Cloud tier deployment (code may exist but not operated)
- ❌ Linear / Gmail / Calendar / DevOps integrations (v1.1+)
- ❌ Sign-up / onboarding wizard (zero signup; CLI prompts when needed)
- ❌ Any LLM-backed feature requiring BYOK (defer until first agent ships in v1.1)

---

## How to help

If you want to contribute, **wait until `CONTRIBUTING.md` lands** (early in this roadmap). For now:

- ⭐ Star the repo to follow along
- 💬 Open a Discussion with feedback or use-case ideas
- 🪲 If you find a real bug in alpha builds, file an Issue (templates land soon)
- 🔒 Report security vulnerabilities privately (see [SECURITY.md](../blob/main/SECURITY.md))

---

_This issue is updated as items complete. Subscribe to follow along._
title: [CLOSED] v1.0.0 roadmap

author: lachiejames
text:
## Roadmap amendments (post #11 resolution)

#11 resolved: **stdio-only MCP transport for v1; no MCP-layer auth.** Roadmap items below need adjustment.

### Drop from v1
- ❌ \`slopweaver token create / list / revoke\` — MCP token CRUD (no longer needed; stdio inherits user trust)
- ❌ \`packages/auth/\` — MCP token CRUD (becomes year-2 cloud-tier work)
- ❌ HTTP MCP transport on \`localhost:60701\` — \`60701\` becomes web-UI-only, not an MCP endpoint

### Reword
- 🔄 \`slopweaver\` (default command) → \"runs the local server with **stdio MCP transport**; \`localhost:60701\` is the web UI only (separate concern from MCP)\"
- 🔄 GitHub OAuth App callback → still needed, but for *user-to-GitHub* OAuth (so SlopWeaver can poll your PRs), not for *MCP-client-to-SlopWeaver* OAuth

### Add to v1
- ➕ **Target SDK \`v1.x\`, not \`main\`** — \`main\` is pre-alpha v2 ([SDK README](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk))
- ➕ **DNS-rebinding + Origin validation** on the localhost:60701 web UI per [MCP transports spec](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports)
- ➕ **MCP Registry submission** as a launch task — \`server.json\` format, namespace verification ([registry docs](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/about))

### Add to year 2 (cloud tier)
- ➕ Streamable HTTP transport (not SSE — backwards-compat only per current spec)
- ➕ OAuth 2.1+PKCE flow
- ➕ Bearer/service-token fallback for CI/headless (Supabase/Neon/GitHub/Linear all do this)

### Effect on \"Pre-launch (founder action items)\"
- The GitHub OAuth App is still needed (for user-to-GitHub auth)
- The Slack App is still needed (same reason)
- No \"MCP OAuth App\" needed at all

### CLI command list now reads:
- \`npx -y slopweaver@latest init\` — first-run setup
- \`slopweaver doctor\` — diagnostics
- \`slopweaver connect github\` — GitHub PAT or OAuth (user → GitHub, not user → SlopWeaver)
- \`slopweaver connect slack\` — Slack bot token (preview)
- ~~\`slopweaver token create / list / revoke\`~~ — **dropped**
- \`slopweaver\` — runs stdio MCP server (registered via \`claude mcp add slopweaver -- slopweaver\`)
- \`slopweaver status\` — service health

Concrete sub-issues filed in the order they should land: see new \`task\` issues in this repo (#18+).

author: lachiejames
text:
## Status snapshot — 2026-05-17

**Done since last update:**
- #34 connect github / connect slack subcommands
- #35 start_session composite tool
- #36 packages/ui + Diagnostics page on localhost:60701
- #40 testing & code-quality rules + reviewer agents
- #41 decision: adopt neverthrow Result pattern
- #42 packages migrated to Result/ResultAsync
- #43 docs: CODEBASE_MAP refresh

**Critical wire-up gap (the dogfood blocker):**
`start_session` currently runs with no pollers — see `apps/mcp-local/src/cli.ts:107-111` and the comment "Pollers are intentionally empty in v1". Tokens stored via `connect` are never read at startup. Three new sub-issues filed to close this gap:

- #44 feat(integrations): add StartSessionPoller adapters for github + slack
- #45 feat(mcp-local): wire integration tokens into start_session pollers
- #46 feat(mcp-server): isolate per-integration poller failures in start_session

Each ships with a codex-orchestration chain file under `.claude/orchestration/v1-wire/` and a `/fix-issue` AI-agent prompt at the bottom of the issue body. Sequence them: #44 → #45 → #46.

**Remaining v1 waves (unchanged):**
- Wave 2 (full tool surface): #38 — get_freshness / catch_me_up / search_work_context
- Wave 3 (smooth first run): #39 — slopweaver init wizard
- Wave 4 (security): #37 — token encryption at rest (decision still open)
- Wave 5 (UI completeness): Session view, Evidence Log Browser, Inbox, Settings (not yet filed as discrete issues)
- Wave 6 (launch collateral): video, MCP Registry, PulseMCP, awesome-mcp, plugin marketplace, Show HN

**Recently closed:**
- #10 Scaffolding & DevOps catch-all — subsumed by #6/#13/#14/#17/#32. Future DevOps work files its own issue.

author: lachiejames
text:
## Status snapshot — 2026-05-17 (dogfood gap closed)

**Wave 1 (dogfood wire-up) — DONE ✓**

The three sub-issues filed earlier today all shipped:

- #44 → PR #47 — StartSessionPoller adapters for github + slack
- #45 → PR #48 — wire integration tokens into start_session pollers
- #46 → PR #50 — isolate per-integration poller failures in start_session

`start_session` now returns real ranked items across connected integrations. The "Pollers are intentionally empty in v1" comment at `apps/mcp-local/src/cli.ts:107-111` is gone. Dogfood-working v1 is real.

**Remaining v1 waves:**

- Wave 2 (full tool surface): #38 — `get_freshness` / `catch_me_up` / `search_work_context` with FTS5 migration
- Wave 3 (smooth first run): #39 — `slopweaver init` wizard
- Wave 4 (security): #37 — token encryption at rest (decision still open — blocker for public launch, not for personal dogfood)
- Wave 5 (UI completeness): Session view, Evidence Log Browser, Inbox, Settings (not yet filed as discrete issues)
- Wave 6 (launch collateral): video, MCP Registry, PulseMCP, awesome-mcp lists, plugin marketplace, Show HN

**Open issues:** #2 (this), #37, #38, #39. Everything else is closed.

text:
Review: REVIEW_REQUIRED · Mergeable: UNKNOWN · Checks: SUCCESS

[![CI](https://img.shields.io/badge/CI-passing-2ea44f?style=flat&logo=githubactions&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/slopweaver/slopweaver/actions/runs/[number]) ![size](https://img.shields.io/badge/size-large-d73a49?style=flat) ![proof](https://img.shields.io/badge/proof-bronze-cd7f32?style=flat)

| 👉 Next | Self-review, then open PR2 — corpus layer + GitHub connector → bronze. |
|---|---|

| | |
|---|---|
| **Problem** | The repo held an old MCP-era prototype. v0.1 needs a clean foundation — plugin scaffold, CLI framework, and a hard "nothing private leaks" guarantee — before any ingestion or retrieval lands. |
| **Solution** | Strip the prototype; add the plugin scaffold, a CLI verb framework (pure `argv → exit-code`, ships `doctor`), and a hygiene gate scanning every file for leak classes + a private denylist. Contributor rules in `AGENTS.md`. Toolchain: TypeScript · Yarn 4 · vitest, MIT. |
| **Proof** | `yarn typecheck` / `test:unit` **12/12** / `hygiene` clean (43 files). [CI green](https://github.com/slopweaver/slopweaver/actions/runs/[number]). Gate proven **cwd-independent** and **blocks a planted denylist term at pre-push** (exit 1). |

Draft — self-review only. Rationale for the non-obvious calls is in the inline self-review comments.

author: lachiejames
text: `node-modules` linker, not PnP — deliberate. PnP's resolution shims are the usual source of tooling incompatibility (editors, `tsx`, `vitest`); node-modules keeps resolution standard. The Yarn version is pinned via `packageManager`, so the release binary isn't vendored into the repo.

author: lachiejames
text: Not a general logger (pino etc.) by design — it's a stdout/stderr **stream-separator** so `slopweaver ask … | grep` sees only data on stdout, never diagnostics. Kept dependency-free; the single injectable `sink` makes it pure-testable without mocks.

author: lachiejames
text: A warnings+errors **accumulator**, not neverthrow's railway `Result<T,E>` — the `warnings[]` channel is why neverthrow isn't a drop-in. Only three helpers; revisit adopting neverthrow if/when PR2–PR3 actually lean on chaining.

author: lachiejames
text: The 'gate paradox' fix: a denylist that scans for org literals would have to *contain* them. So the public gate (this file) detects only generic leak **classes** and names no org; the actual org words live solely in `$SLOPWEAVER_HOME/hygiene-denylist.txt` (uncommitted), read at runtime here.

author: lachiejames
text: One-line `@AGENTS.md` import. Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md`; other agents read `AGENTS.md`. Keeping AGENTS.md as the single source and importing it avoids drift between two rule files.

author: lachiejames
text: Self-review — rationale for the non-obvious calls, on the lines they explain.

👉 NextSelf-review, then PR3 — derive + distil (silver → gold).
ProblemSlopweaver has a CLI shell but no data. It needs the ingest foundation — a source-agnostic record, a GitHub connector, and a local bronze store — so the later stages (silver/gold, ask) have a corpus to work from.
SolutionAdds the CorpusRecord atom, config (home + repo-from-git-remote + gh-first token), a two-lane GitHub connector (REST search → GraphQL activity enrichment → pure projection), the bronze pipeline (redact → fingerprint-dedup → JSONL + watermark), a fail-closed reader, and slopweaver refresh.
ProofSilver — screenshot of the live run (below): slopweaver refresh --repo slopweaver/slopweaver dogfooded on this repo — command → per-item GraphQL enrichment (#87…#54, 5/5) → wrote 27 new record(s) to bronze.
55 unit tests (36 new) · yarn typecheck + hygiene clean · idempotent (immediate re-run dedups all) · CI green: run.

author: lachiejames
text: No `visibility`/ownership field, deliberately. v0.1 has no private/owner-only lane, so every record is equally readable — which also means the reader never has to fail-closed on an unparseable access tag. The field lands only if/when a private lane does.

author: lachiejames
text: gh-first token chain: an explicit `GITHUB_TOKEN`/`GH_TOKEN` wins, else the existing `gh` CLI login. Most users need no token at all — killing the PAT-setup friction that would otherwise gate onboarding.

author: lachiejames
text: The cursor is the max OBSERVED `tsIso`, not the window's `until`. Advancing to `until` would skip any record that lands later carrying an earlier timestamp (a gap). Merge is MAX at the leaf, so a narrower re-run can never move a cursor backwards.

author: lachiejames
text: The content fingerprint blanks `tsIso`, so re-capturing identical content at a new time doesn't count as a change (idempotent). A record writes only when its fingerprint is new for its `sourceId` AND its `tsIso` ≥ the newest stored — update-aware, and immune to a stale re-fetch overwriting a newer line.

author: lachiejames
text: Asymmetric failure handling on purpose: a **search** failure is fatal (`err`) so we never silently persist a partial window, but a per-item **enrichment** failure is swallowed (the atom ships without its activity) so one bad item can't sink a whole refresh.

author: lachiejames
text: `resolveCorpusDir` refuses a dir that's absent or holds no `.jsonl`. Closes the footgun where a wrong `--corpus`/home path silently read as "0 records" (looked like no data when it was really a bad path).

author: lachiejames
text: This hash IS the incrementality engine: it changes iff a batch's source/container/record-ids/text change, so a re-run serves unchanged batches from cache and re-calls the model ONLY for changed ones. Result: LLM spend scales with the bronze delta, not the corpus size.

author: lachiejames
text: Hub-token cap: a token held by >200 records is a status enum / templated URL / generic mention whose O(H²) clique is pure noise (and would blow past V8's Map-size ceiling on a big corpus). Real shared entities never fan out that far, so dropping them loses no signal.

author: lachiejames
text: Recency is measured against the NEWEST record in the corpus, not wall-clock now — so opportunity scores are stable/deterministic across runs (re-deriving the same corpus tomorrow yields identical scores), which keeps the silver artifacts diff-clean.

author: lachiejames
text: A digest point with no citation is DROPPED (and a digest with neither a summary nor any cited point errs → retry). Gold is only as trustworthy as its grounding; this refuses ungrounded model claims rather than surfacing them.

author: lachiejames
text: Pass-through for v0.1: GitHub authors are already human handles, so an unknown token returns verbatim and the map is empty by default (empty template shipped, roster never committed). The cross-platform `githubMatch`/people-registry (with its `ORG_SUFFIX` org coupling) is deliberately NOT ported — it's Slack/Linear correlation, out of GitHub-only scope.

text:
_🩺 Stability & Availability_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Drain or ignore stderr to prevent pipe-buffer deadlock.**

`spawn` uses default stdio (`'pipe'` for all three), but stderr is never read. If the `claude` CLI writes more than the OS pipe buffer (~64KB) to stderr, the process blocks indefinitely. The timeout would eventually kill it, but only after the full 150s budget.

<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-        const child = spawn('claude', ['-p', '--output-format', 'json'], { detached: true })
+        const child = spawn('claude', ['-p', '--output-format', 'json'], { detached: true, stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'inherit'] })
```

Or capture stderr for diagnostics:
```diff
+        let stderr = ''
+        child.stderr.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => { stderr += chunk.toString('utf8') })
```
and include `stderr` in the error message on non-zero exit.
</details>

author: lachiejames
text: The cache key is `sourceId` + this content hash, so an unchanged record reuses its vector, an edit re-embeds, and a model/char-cap change invalidates everything. `buildVectorIndex` is read-only by default (persists only on a full-corpus build) because `save` overwrites the whole file — persisting a query-time subset would truncate the cache.

author: lachiejames
text: NDJSON, not one JSON array, on purpose: at scale a single `JSON.stringify`/`readFileSync` of ~100k×768 vectors crosses V8's ~512MB max-string limit and throws — which would silently, permanently degrade semantic search to BM25. Wrong-dimension rows are dropped on load (re-embedded), never fed to cosine.

author: lachiejames
text: The relevance floor cuts a candidate only when BOTH sides are weak, and ONLY when the semantic side actually contributed — so a strong lexical-only hit is never floored away, and with the embedder unavailable (BM25-only) the floor is disabled entirely. Fail-soft is structural, not a special case.

author: lachiejames
text: Two grounding guards: a model citation survives only if its token is one the slice actually offered (`evidenceTokens`), and any inline `(TOKEN)` whose token didn't survive is stripped from the prose. So the answer can never show a citation it can't back — a hallucinated ticket/PR reference is dropped, not surfaced.

author: lachiejames
text: Behaviour change to PR1's router: an unknown verb token now routes to the noun's `''` default handler (if it has one) instead of rendering usage. This is what lets `slopweaver ask <free text>` work — the default handler owns the whole tail as arguments. Nouns without a `''` default still fall through to usage for an unknown verb (covered by the new `plain`-noun test).

author: lachiejames
text: This is the cross-platform embeddings fix. `@xenova/transformers` hard-depends on `sharp` (a native, image-only lib we never use) and imports it eagerly, so a machine without sharp's native binary couldn't even import the embedder. Stubbing sharp via a Yarn `resolution` (portal→this no-op) removes the only hard native dep; inference then runs on `onnxruntime-web` (pure WASM). Net: no native build on ANY platform — the plugin works the same on Mac/Windows/Linux/any arch. Answers your "how does this work in a plugin / non-Mac" question: it now does.

author: lachiejames
text: `persist: true` (fixed here): slopweaver always indexes the FULL corpus, so persisting the vector cache is safe and gives incremental reuse. Without it every query re-embedded the whole corpus (62 records = ~23s each). Now: first query embeds + caches (~23s/62 records), every later query reuses in ~1s and only re-embeds new/changed records.

author: lachiejames
text: The bug: evidence was ONLY each sliced record's own cite token, but gold digests summarise + reference other records — so a gold-grounded answer cited `#88`/`#88:comment:2` (read from the gold text), none of which were the gold record's own token, and ALL citations got stripped → used:0. Fix: evidence now also includes cite-shaped ids MENTIONED inside sliced records (mapped to that record's URL). Grounding integrity holds — a truly invented id, in no sliced record, is still dropped. Citations are also gathered from inline prose (not just citations[]) and deduped by URL. Verified live: 3/3 runs exit 0 with citations present, no dups.

author: lachiejames
text: Exit code now keys off `retrieved` (did the query match any records) rather than `used` (did citations survive). A substantive answer with zero surviving citations is a real answer → exit 0, not EXIT_EXPECTED_EMPTY (which wrongly signalled 'nothing matched' and would drop the run out of a caller's reliability stats).

author: lachiejames
text: Self-review — the packaging design, reproducible via the clean-layout sim in the Proof. (The bin/slopweaver launcher — unchanged since PR1, so not inline-commentable here — bridges dev vs cache-install layouts: it links ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/cli-deps/node_modules into the plugin root so tsx resolves deps, and verifies tsx is executable so a half-cleaned node_modules can't wedge it.)

author: lachiejames
text: Follow-up: fixes the ask citation/exit-code bug the first acceptance run surfaced.
